tarte-a-loignon-french-onion-pie.name: Tarte A L'oignon (french Onion Pie)
instructions: Preheat oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Place the bacon into a skillet, and cook over medium heat until browned. Remove bacon from skillet, reserving 4 tablespoons bacon fat, and drain on paper towels.

Place the onions into the same skillet with the bacon fat, and cook over medium-high heat until evenly browned, about 8 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Combine the milk and cream in a bowl. Sprinkle the flour over the onions, and stir to blend. Stir in the milk mixture. Cook and stir over medium heat until the mixture thickens. Remove from heat, stir in the bacon, and set aside to cool 10 minutes.

Beat the eggs in a mixing bowl until light colored and frothy. Stir a spoonful of the onion mixture into the eggs. Add another spoonful of the onion mixture, and continue stirring. Repeat, until all the onions have been stirred into the eggs and are thoroughly blended. Pour the mixture into the prepared pie shell. Sprinkle with nutmeg.
Bake in preheated oven until the crust is lightly browned, about 20 minutes. Remove from the oven and cool 5 minutes before serving.

thai-style-iced-tea.name: Thai-style Iced Tea
instructions: Combine 10 star anise pods, 3 tablespoons sugar, and 1 cup water in a small saucepan. Scrape in seeds from 1 vanilla bean; add bean. Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer 10 minutes. Transfer to a large pitcher. Add 15 black tea bags and 7 cups cold water. Cover; chill at least 4 hours. Strain into another large pitcher. Stir in 1/2 cup heavy cream and 1/2 cup sweetened condensed milk until dissolved. Serve over ice.

the-creamiest-flan.name: The Creamiest Flan
instructions: Place a rack in middle of oven; preheat to 350°. Set a 9x5" loaf pan inside a 13x9" baking dish. Bring 1 cup (200 g) sugar, 1 Tbsp. light corn syrup, and 3 Tbsp. water to a boil in a small saucepan over medium heat, swirling pan occasionally (do not stir). Cook until caramel is dark amber, about 4 minutes. Carefully pour into loaf pan and let sit until set, about 10 minutes.

Using an electric mixer on medium speed, beat 8 oz. cream cheese, room temperature, and ½ cup (100 g) sugar in a medium bowl until combined. Beat in 4 large egg yolks and 3 large eggs; scrape down sides of bowl. Scrape in vanilla seeds from 2 vanilla beans; reserve pods for another use. Reduce mixer speed to low, add one 14-oz. can sweetened condensed milk, one 12-oz. can evaporated milk, and 1 cup whole milk, and beat until custard is smooth, about 1 minute.

Pour custard into loaf pan. Pour water into baking dish to come ¾" up sides of loaf pan. Bake flan until pale golden on top and just set in the center but still a little wobbly when jiggled, about 1½ hours. Remove loaf pan from baking dish and transfer to a wire rack. Let flan cool in pan 15 minutes, then chill at least 3 hours and, preferably, up to 12 hours.
Just before serving, run a small knife around edges of flan. Set a platter on top of loaf pan and carefully invert flan onto platter.

the-new-stuffing.name: The New Stuffing

ingredients.prep: cut into pieces, divided, plus more

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°. Butter a shallow 3-qt. baking dish and a sheet of foil. Place bread in a very large bowl.
Toast hazelnuts on a rimmed baking sheet, tossing once, until golden brown, 10–12 minutes. Let cool; coarsely chop, then add to bowl with bread.

Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add sausage and cook, stirring occasionally and breaking into small pieces with a spoon, until browned and cooked through, 7–10 minutes. Transfer to bowl with bread with a slotted spoon.
Add onions, celery, fennel, and sage to skillet, season with salt and pepper, and cook, stirring often, until onions are golden brown and soft, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to bowl with bread.
Reduce heat to medium and cook wine in skillet, scraping up any browned bits, until almost all evaporated, about 1 minute. Add ½ cup butter; cook, stirring, until melted. Drizzle over bread mixture.

Whisk eggs and 2 cups stock in a medium bowl; pour over bread mixture. Season with salt and pepper and toss, adding more stock ¼-cupful at a time as needed (you may not use it all), until combined and bread is hydrated. Transfer to prepared baking dish and dot with remaining ¼ cup butter.

Cover with buttered foil; bake until a paring knife inserted into the center comes out hot, 30–35 minutes. Increase oven temperature to 450°. Uncover and bake until top is golden brown and crisp, 20–25 minutes. Let sit 10 minutes before serving.
Do Ahead: Stuffing can be assembled 1 day ahead. Cover and chill. 

Editor’s note: Head this way for more of our best Thanksgiving stuffing recipes →

the-strawberry-muddle.name: The Strawberry Muddle
instructions: Stir sugar and 1/2 cup water in heavy small saucepan over medium-low heat until sugar dissolves. Increase heat and bring to boil. Cool syrup.

Divide strawberries among six 6- to 8-ounce glasses; add 1 tablespoon syrup to each and mash with muddler or handle of wooden spoon. Add lemon slice to each and mash to release flavor. Add several ice cubes to each glass, then fill with Prosecco.

the-waffle.name: The Waffle
instructions: Preheat a waffle iron to medium-high.

In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Make a well in the center and pour in the milk, buttermilk, melted butter, vanilla, and egg yolks. Whisk until the batter just comes together (there will be some lumps).

Add the egg whites to a medium bowl and beat with an electric hand mixer until foamy. With the mixer running, gradually add the sugar and continue beating until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the egg whites into the batter until just combined.

Spray the heated waffle iron with nonstick cooking spray. Ladle the batter into the waffle iron, close the lid, and cook according to manufacturer’s instructions until the waffle is golden brown and crisp, 5–6 minutes. Transfer the waffle to a plate and repeat with the remaining batter.
Serve the waffles hot, with butter and maple syrup alongside.
Enjoy!

thracian-style-lamb-tandoori-lezzet.name: Thracian Style Lamb Tandoori
instructions: 1. Refine lamb meat with olive oil, grain pepper, bay leaf and salt.
2. Bake 140 hours in the oven.
3. For inner rice, roast the peanuts.
4. Add the onions you eat and continue roasting.
5. Add Pirinci and add spices after a bit more roasting.
6. Add water and drop it out.
7. In addition to the grapes close to pee and leave for 20 minutes.
8. Serve meat with eggplant and inner rice, which is boiled in butter.
9. You can also try the classic lamb tandir recipe, known for its light weight and decorated the main dishes.

toasted-garlic-beef-stock.name: Toasted Garlic-beef Stock
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°. Roast bones on a parchment-lined rimmed baking sheet 30 minutes. Arrange celery, onion, and carrots on sheet; roast 10 minutes. Spread tomato paste over bones and vegetables and roast 5 minutes more; let cool.

Meanwhile, heat oil in a small saucepan over medium and cook garlic, shaking pan occasionally, until golden, about 5 minutes. Immediately strain oil through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl; set aside garlic. Set aside oil for Ditalini Risotto and other uses.

Transfer bones and vegetables to a large pot; pour in cold water to cover. Add herb stems, bay leaves, peppercorns, coriander seeds, and reserved garlic. Bring to a boil over medium heat. Reduce heat to low and simmer, skimming fat and foam from surface, until caramel colored and flavorful, about 3 hours. Strain stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, pressing on solids; discard solids.
Do Ahead: Stock can be made 3 days ahead. Let cool; cover and chill, or freeze up to 3 months.

tomato-crumbs-with-fried-fish-teleculina.name: Tomato Crumbs With Fried Fish
tomato-crumbs-with-fried-fish-teleculina.original_name: Migas de tomate com peixe frito
instructions: Cut the bread into small pieces, lie in a bowl, add cold water and let it fall apart. Drain the water well and reserve the bread.

Peel and pinch the onions and garlic and praise them in a frying pan with the olive oil. Add the tomato and let it saute. Then add the bread, season with salt and pepper and wrap it very well, until you get one prepared with the consistency of migas.

Cut the fish into regular pieces, season with salt and pepper and lemon juice. Pass through flour, egg beaten and bread grated and fry in abundant and very hot oil. Drain on absorbent paper and serve with tomato migas.

tomato-pesto-salmon.name: Tomato Pesto Salmon
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400˚F (200˚C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Add the zucchini to the prepared baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Lay the salmon on top the zucchini, then spread the pesto on top of the salmon. Top with the tomatoes.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, until the vegetables are tender and the salmon flakes easily when nudged with a fork.
Enjoy!

torrijas-with-syrup-in-air-fryer-pequere.name: Torrijas With Syrup In Air Fryer
torrijas-with-syrup-in-air-fryer-pequere.original_name: Torrijas con almíbar en freidora de aire
instructions: In a bowl we put water next to the sugar and cinnamon branch (if you want to do more syrup multiplies quantity)
Let's remove with some rods till the sugar completely dissolve. When the water starts to boil, we lower our fire and let them cook for about 15 minutes.
We withdraw from the fire and let them cool before bathing their calves.

tortilla-espanola.name: Tortilla Española
instructions: Heat 1 Tbsp. oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add onion, season with salt, and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and dark brown, 35–40 minutes. Let cool slightly.

Meanwhile, heat potatoes and remaining 2 cups oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat until oil around potatoes begins to bubble; reduce heat to medium and cook until potatoes are tender but have not taken on any color, 10–12 minutes. Drain potatoes, reserving oil. Season potatoes with salt and let cool slightly.
Combine eggs, onion, potatoes, and ¼ cup reserved potato cooking oil in a large bowl and gently beat with a fork.

Heat 3 Tbsp. reserved potato cooking oil in a 10” nonstick skillet over medium heat (reserve remaining oil for another use). Add egg mixture and cook, lifting at edge and tilting skillet to let uncooked egg run underneath, until bottom and edge of tortilla are set but center is still wet.
Set a large plate on top of skillet. Swiftly invert tortilla onto plate, then slide back into skillet, cooked side up. Cook until center is just set, about 2 minutes longer. Cut into wedges.

traditional-cod-stew-teleculinaria.name: Traditional Cod Stew
instructions: Peel the potatoes, onions and garlic and cut them into slices, as well as peppers, previously cleaned from seeds. Book the potatoes in water. Remove skin and seeds from tomatoes and cut them into cubes. Also cut the cod posts into two or three pieces.

In a wide pot, put the ingredients in layers, starting with the onion, garlic, and blond leaves. Then add the potatoes, cod, peppers, and chilli cut into slices (without seeds). Season with wine, olive oil, tomato previously mixed with concentrate, salt and pepper.

Add the parsley and thyme branches, take them to the fire and let them cook for 25 minutes. As soon as it boils, lower the heat and let the cooking finish, stirring the pot from time to time. Remove from the fire and serve immediately.

traditional-lasagna-from-astypalaia-argi.name: Traditional Lasagna From Astypalaia

ingredients.item: 800 gr of green (flower or fresh myzithra)
800 gr of green (flower or fresh myzithra)

instructions: For the handmade stuffed lasagna, in a bowl, mix first all the liquid ingredients for the dough and slowly add the flour until it becomes a hard dough.
In a briquet, dry the saffron (or saphor). Beat it in the mortar to powder and dissolve it in a little hot water. Add salt and pepper.
Open thin leaf and fill in the shape of caramel.
Boil in salted water until they rise to the surface.
Strain and pour with hot butter.
Thank you very much for sharing this traditional recipe from Astypalea.

tricolor-chicken-flautas-recipe-comedera.name: Tricolor Chicken Flautas Recipe
tricolor-chicken-flautas-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de flautas tricolor de pollo

ingredients.item: 500 gr de pollo cocido y desmechado
1 taza de queso panela o fresco

instructions: Para la salsa roja, cuece en una olla con suficiente agua a fuego medio el tomate, la cebolla y el ajo durante 5 minutos.
When cooked, remove from water and add to the blender the vegetables with chipotle chili, salt and pepper to taste. Rig well and reserve.
For the green salsa, add Jalapeño chiles, avocados, tomatoes, onion, garlic, salt and pepper to the blender. Triture up to a homogenous blend. Reserve.
On an omelette of corn, place the pitted chicken and roll onto itself. You can put picadient onto the omelette to prevent them from opening at the time of frying. Repeat with all tortillas.

In a frying pan with medium heat heats up a cup of oil. When you have the indicated temperature, frying the flutes to a uniform golden, sometimes spinning. When ready, remove and let them slip into a paper napkin.
Serve 3 flutes on a plate, baits with green salsa, followed by sour cream and finally with red salsa. Add panela and decorate with a cilantro.

triple-chocolate-cake-argiro.name: Triple Chocolate Cake

ingredients.item: 110 Gruverture of very good quality (in pieces)
500 ml cream beaten in thick form
110 Gruverture of very good quality (in pieces)
500 ml cream beaten in thick form

instructions: Beat the eggs with the sugar in the mixer for 7'-8' to white and fluffy.
Add the sifted flour to the sifted cocoa powder and gently fold it with spatula so that we don't lose the air we trapped in the beaten eggs.
Butter and flour a ring or baking pan with a diameter of 23 cm.
Bake in a well preheated ocean at 180°C in air, on the medium rack, for 20′-25′′, to unstick from the walls.
When it is cooked, let it pass 5–6–1, take off and let it cool complete.
Warm all materials in low fire to unite.
We don't boil them, or they'll cut the milk.
Let it cool.
In a small pot, in very low heat melt the bitter chocolate with the cream.
We do the same with white chocolate.
Let them cool a little.
Beat the 500 ml cream in the mixer in thick form.
Take 2/3 of the thick cream and gently combine with the melted white chocolate.
Combine the remaining whipped cream (1/3) with the melted bitter chocolate.
Leave both mousses in the refrigerator for 15 minutes.
In the form that we baked the cake, we make a membrane (to spare enough of the walls).
Split the cake in two places.
We put in the form a piece.
Rub it with a brush, with enough syrup, to water well.
Spread up half the white chocolate mousse.
Cover with all the bitter chocolate mousse.
Finally, we make a layer with the rest of the white.
Cover with the second cake.
We syrup well with a brush.
Turn the membranes that protrude from the form and cover the entire surface well.
Place in the refrigerator for 4 hours – even better for one night.
Melt milk chocolate with cream over very low heat.
Unburden, remove the membranes and place on a tour.
